title: Dipterostele helleri
taxonomy: Dipterostele helleri is classified as a species of orchid, specifically within the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Dipterostele and is native to Asia. The species is further classified into different subspecies based on its geographical distribution and morphological characteristics. The classification of Dipterostele helleri is based on the taxonomic principles of Carl Linnaeus and later modifications. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships among different species of orchids.

intro: Dipterostele helleri, also known as Heller's dipterostele, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various diseases such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dipterostele helleri include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Dipterostele helleri is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, particularly those related to respiratory and digestive systems.
